detaileddescription.para: MP4 format.
VP8/VORBIS data in a WEBM container.
3gpp format.
HEIC data in a HEIF container.
Opus data in a OGG container.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
This enumarates list of all the possibly supported output formats for the muxer across all the releases/Platform.
Use #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats to query for all supported Output formats on the Platform.
Camera video source.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Media recorder video source.
Recording voice.
Recording ambient sounds.
Recording digital sounds.
Mixed reality mode: digital + ambient.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Media recorder audio source.
Media recorder error.
Media recorder info.
Media recorder track error.
Media recorder track info.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Media recorder events.
Max duration of the clip is reached.
Max file size is reached.
Max file size is approaching.
Next output file has started.
The info about completion status.
The info about progress in time.
Track info.
The info about track duration.
The time to measure the max chunk duration.
The info about encoded frames.
The time to measure how well the audio and video track data interleaved.
The time to measure system response.
The time used to compensate for initial A/V sync.
Total number of bytes of the media data.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
3gpp format.
MP4 format.
AMR NB.
AMR WB.
AAC_ADIF.
AAC_ADTS.
Stream over a socket, limited to a single stream.
H.264/AAC data encapsulated in MPEG2/TS.
VP8/VORBIS data in a WEBM container.
HEIC data in a HEIF container.
Opus data in a OGG container.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
H263. This format has support for software encoder.
H264. This format has support for hardware encoder.
MPEG4 SP. This format has support for software encoder.
VP8. This format has support for software encoder.
HEVC. This format has support for hardware encoder.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
AMR NB.
AMR WB.
AAC.
HE AAC.
AAC ELD.
Vorbis.
Opus.
Ensure enum is represented as 32 bits.
This enumarates list of all the possibly supported output formats for the muxer across all the releases/Platform.
Use #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats to query for all supported Output formats on the Platform.
Media recorder video source.
Media recorder audio source.
Media recorder events.

memberdef.definition: MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat const char* const size_t* out_mime_list_size
memberdef.name: out_mime_list_size

memberdef.argsstring: ((deprecated("Use standard Android SDK and NDK APIs."))) struct MLMediaMuxerSampleData
memberdef.name: __attribute__

param.type: (deprecated("Use standard Android SDK and NDK APIs."))

briefdescription.para: Data type that encapsulates sample data to be written in to MLMediaMuxer.

detaileddescription.para: Stop muxing.
Writes an encoded sample into the muxer.
Start muxing.
Set and store the geodata (latitude and longitude) in the output file.
Set the orientation hint for output video playback.
Add a track with given format information.
Configure the MLMediaMuxer for a given Output format with output path.
Get a list of all the supported mime-types for a given Media Muxer Output Format on the Platform.
Get a list of all the supported Media Muxer Output Formats on the Platform.
Release the MLMediaMuxer instance.
Create a MLMediaMuxer instance.

parameterdescription.para: Upon successful return will point to handle to the created MLMediaMuxer. Or else, it will point to ML_INVALID_HANDLE.
MLMediaMuxer object creation failed with resource allocation failure.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was created successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was released successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: User doesn't own the memory/buffer returned from this API and MUST NOT be freeing or releasing the out_format_list.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
Pointer to MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat. This should not be NULL.
The size of the returned out_format_list. This should not be NULL.
If one of the parameters are invalid.
Successfully retrieved all the supported Output Formats.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: User doesn't own the memory/buffer returned from this API and MUST NOT be freeing or releasing the out_mime_list.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The Output format, to which we would like to know all the supported mime-types. The format should be one returned from #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats.
Pointer to array of mime-type (char array). This should not be NULL.
The size of the returned out_mime_list. This should not be NULL.
If one of the parameters is invalid.
Successfully retrieved all the supported Output Formats.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The format of the muxed output media file. The format should be one returned from #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedOutputFormats.
The linux-style path for the output media file. This value cannot be empty or NULL.
Failed to configure the MLMediaMuxer object with resource allocation failure.
One of the parameters is invalid.
MLMediaMuxer object was configured successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
If the format is not supported.

detaileddescription.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.
The following media format keys are supported for Audio/Video and Metadata tracks.

sect1.title: | Audio Track | Video Track | Metadata Track |
sect1.para: | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Sample_Rate | MLMediaFormat_Key_Width | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Channel_Count | MLMediaFormat_Key_Height | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD0 | MLMediaFormat_Key_Bt_Rate | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD1 | MLMediaFormat_Key_Frame_Rate | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Range | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Standard | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_Color_Transfer | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_HDR_Static_Info | | | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD0 | |

sect1.title: | | MLMediaFormat_Key_CSD1 | |
sect1.para: #MLMediaMuxerGetSupportedMimes can be used to query for all the supported MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime for a given Output Format.

While MLMediaFormat_Key_Mime is must for all tracks, some of the keys aren't mandatory. For Audio track, MLMediaFormat_Key_SAMPLE_RATE and MLMediaFormat_Key_CHANNEL_COUNT is must. For Video track, MLMediaFormat_Key_WIDTH anb MLMediaFormat_Key_HEIGHT are must.
Requierment of providing csd (Codec Specific Data) through MediaFormat is optional for some audio/video mimes where as it is mandatory for some other ones (for example vorbis).
With mp4 container following audio/video and metadata mime types can be supported.

sect1.title: | Audio Mimes | Video Mimes | Metadata Mimes |
sect1.para: | audio/3gpp | video/3gpp | application/ | | audio/mp4a-latm | video/mp4v-es | image/vnd.android.heic | | audio/amr-wb | video/avc | |

sect1.title: | | video/hevc | |
sect1.para: With Webm container following audio/video mime types can be supported.

sect1.title: | Audio Mimes | Video Mimes |
sect1.para: | audio/vorbis | video/x-vnd.on2.vp8 |

sect1.title: | audio/opus | video/x-vnd.on2.vp9 |
sect1.para: With Ogg container, only supported mime is "audio/opus".
If a mime passed through the MLMediaFormat is not supported, it will return MLMediaResult_Unsupported.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
MLHandle to the MLMediaFormat that contains the track's format.
Upon successful return, this will conatin the index of the track added.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.
If one of the keys in the format is not supported.

sect1.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.

Calling this method will not rotate the video frame when muxer is generating the file, but add a composition matrix containing the rotation angle in the output video if the output format is MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat_MPEG4 so that a video player can choose the proper orientation for playback.
Note that some video players may choose to ignore the composition matrix in a video during playback. By default, the rotation degree is 0.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The rotation degrees. It has to be either 0, 90, 180 or 270.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: This should be called before #MLMediaMuxerStart() and after #MLMediaMuxerConfigure.
The geodata is stored in udta box if the output format is MLMediaMuxerOutputFormat_MPEG4, and is ignored for other output formats. The geodata is stored according to ISO-6709 standard.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The latitude in degree. Its value must be in the range [-90, 90].
The longitude in degree. Its value must be in the range [-180, 180].
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: Make sure all the tracks have been added (#MLMediaMuxerAddTrack) before calling this.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: The application needs to make sure that the samples are written into the right tracks. Also, it needs to make sure the samples for each track are written in chronological order (e.g. in the order they are provided by the encoder).

For MPEG4 media format, the duration of the last sample in a track can be set by passing an additional empty buffer) with MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_EOS flag and a suitable presentation timestamp set in time_us (of MLMediaMuxerSampleData structure) as the last sample of that track. This last sample's presentation timestamp shall be a sum of the presentation timestamp and the duration preferred for the original last sample. If no explicit END_OF_STREAM sample was passed, then the duration of the last sample would be the same as that of the sample before that.
The buffer can be reused once this method returns.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
The sample data to be written encapsulated in MLMediaMuxerSampleData.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

sect1.para: Once the muxer stops, it can not be restarted.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MLMediaMuxer instance.
One of the parameters is invalid.
If the operation completes successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
The operation is invalid.

inbodydescription.para: The track index number, to which to write the sample data into. This should be one of the number returned by #MLMediaMuxerAddTrack.
The buffer's time stamp in microseconds.

The flag about the data. The following flags are supported: MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_KeyFrame: if its a sync/key frame. MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_EOS: To notify end of stream. MLMediaCodecBufferFlag_MuxerData: if it is muxer data (only Exif data block is supported).

parameterdescription.para: SampleData defined by MLMediaMuxerSampleData that needs to be initialized.

memberdef.argsstring: ((deprecated("Use standard Android SDK APIs."))) MLResult MLMediaRecorderCreate(MLHandle *out_handle)
memberdef.name: __attribute__

detaileddescription.para: Restarts the MediaRecorder to its idle state.
Release the surface that was returned by MLMediaRecorderGetInputSurface.
Stops recording.
Returns the maximum absolute amplitude that was sampled since the last call to this method.
Gets the input surface to record from when using SURFACE video source.
Begins capturing and encoding data to the specified file.
Prepares the recorder to begin capturing and encoding data for input mediaformat.
Sets the GEO location for recording.
Sets the maximum filesize (in bytes) of the recording session.
Sets the maximum duration (in ms) of the recording session.
Sets the audio encoder to be used for recording.
Sets the video encoder to be used for recording.
Sets the format of the output file produced during recording.
Set MediaRecorder audio source.
Sets MediaRecorder default video source.
Sets the MediaRecorderListener object that will receive MediaRecorder notifications.
Sets the path of the output file to be produced.
Pass in the file descriptor of the file to be written.
Destroy a MediaRecorder object.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the new MediaRecorder object created. Only valid if result is MLResult_Ok.
Could not allocate internal MediaRecorder.
Passed out_handle was NULL.
MediaRecorder was created successfully.
Handle to the media recorder object to destroy.
Media recorder handle was not found.
MediaRecorder was destroyed successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() but before MLMediaRecorderprepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder instance.
The file descriptor for the file you want to write into.
Passed fd does no refer to a valid,open file descriptor or 'media_recorder' was NULL.
Data source was correctly set to passed file descriptor.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() but before MLMediaRecorderprepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder instance.
The linux-style path of the file. Must be a valid and existing path.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Data source was correctly set to passed file path.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
Set of event callbacks. Can be set to NULL to unset the callbacks altogether.
Custom data to be returned when any callback is fired.
Media recorder handle was not found.
The specified callbacks were successfully set/unset.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Cannot be called twice (without calling MLMediaRecorderReset() in between) because it triggers internal initialization.
Current implementation supports only camera as video source from MediaRecorderVideoSource.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MediaRecorderVideoSource.
Media recorder handle was not found or in_video_source is not MLMediaRecorderVideoSource_Camera.
Set default video source successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Cannot be called twice (without calling MLMediaRecorderReset() in between) because it triggers internal initialization.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MediaRecorderAudioSource.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set audio source successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MLMediaRecorderOutputFormat.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set output format successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: If this method is not called, the output file will not contain an video track. Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare().
The video source is always set to camera by default.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the VideoEncoder to use.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set video encoder successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
requested width height is not supported.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: If this method is not called, the output file will not contain an audio track. Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Type of the MLMediaRecorderAudioEncoder to use.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Set audio encoder successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). After recording reaches the specified duration, a notification will be sent via the callback with a MLMediaRecorderInfo code of MLMediaRecorderInfo_MaxDurationReached and recording will be stopped. Stopping happens asynchronously, there is no guarantee that the recorder will have stopped by the time the listener is notified.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
The maximum duration in ms (if zero or negative, disables the duration limit).
Media recorder handle was not found.
Max duration was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para:
API Level:\n 20
Call this after MLMediaRecorderSetOutputFormat() and before MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). After recording reaches the specified filesize, a notification will be sent via the callback with a MLMediaRecorderInfo code of MLMediaRecorderInfo_MaxFileSizeReached and recording will be stopped. Stopping happens asynchronously, there is no guarantee that the recorder will have stopped by the time the listener is notified.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
MaxFilesizeBytes The maximum filesize in bytes (if zero or negative, disables the limit).
Media recorder handle was not found.
Max file size was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the media recorder.
The 10000 multiplies latitude of location.
The 10000 multiplies longitude of location.
Media recorder handle was not found.
GEO location was successfully set.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: This method must be called after setting up the desired audio and video sources, encoders, but before start.

API Level:\n 20
media_recorder
Handle to the media recorder.
format
MLHandle to the MediaFormat. MediaFormat object can be created with:
#MLMediaFormatCreateVideo if we want to record video only.
#MLMediaFormatCreateAudio if we want to record audio only.
#MLMediaFormatCreate if we want to record both video and audio.
Following parameters can be added to MediaFormat handle. Audio Specific parametes:
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Bit_Rate for audio "bitrate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Channel_Count for audio "channel-count".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Sample_Rate for audio "sample-rate".

Video Specific parametes:
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Width for video frame "width".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Height for video frame "height".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Frame_Rate for video "frame-rate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_Parameter_Video_Bitrate for "video-bitrate".
#MLMediaFormat_Key_I_Frame_Interval for "i-frame-interval".

parameterdescription.para: Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was prepared successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderPrepare(). The apps should not start another recording session during recording.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was started successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: May only be called after MLMediaRecorderPrepare. Frames rendered to the producer before MLMediaRecorderStart() is called will be discarded.
When using an input surface, there are no accessible input buffers, as buffers are automatically passed from the other modules to this surface.

The returned input surface can also be passed as a destination surface to
a video/mixed reality video capture session when calling MLCameraPrepareCapture().

Captured raw video frames will be consumed directly as input to an encoder without copying. Caller of this API should release the surface using MLMediaRecorderReleaseInputSurface() on the Surface handle after usage.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the Media Recorder instance.
Handle to the created input native surface.
Media recorder handle was not found.
input surface was correctly returned.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this only after the MLMediaRecorderSetAudioSource().

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaRecorder.
The maximum absolute amplitude measured since the last call, or 0 when called for the first time.
Mmedia recorder handle was not found.
Maximum amplitude was returned successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

detaileddescription.para: Call this after MLMediaRecorderStart(). Once recording is stopped, you will have to configure it again as if it has just been constructed.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the MediaRecorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was stopped successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.
Handle to the MediaRecorder.
Handle to the internal surface object.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Surface object was successfully released.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
MLMediaCodec was not initialized.

detaileddescription.para: After calling this method, you will have to configure it again as if it had just been constructed.

parameterdescription.para: Handle to the media recorder.
Media recorder handle was not found.
Recorder was resetted successfully.
The operation failed with an unspecified error.
Method was called from the wrong state.

inbodydescription.para: Holds valid track id only for track specific info (returned via on_track_info callback.). Holds 0 for the generic info (returned via on_info callback). Refer to MLMediaRecorderInfo for the info.
User data as passed to MLMediaRecorderSetEventCallbacks.

Holds valid track id only for track specific errors (returned via on_track_error callback). Holds 0 for the generic errors (returned via on_error callback). Refer to MLMediaRecorderError for the errors.
User data as passed to MLMediaRecorderSetEventCallbacks.
MediaRecorder received a general info/warning message.

media_recorder
Handle is a MediaRecorder instance on which callback was set.
pointer
to MLMediaRecorderOnInfo.
MediaRecorder received a track-related info/warning message.

media_recorder
Handle is a MediaRecorder instance on which callback was set.
track_info
pointer to MLMediaRecorderOnInfo.
MediaRecorder received a general error message.

media_recorder
Handle is a MediaRecorder instance on which callback was set.
pointer
to MLMediaRecorderOnError.
MediaRecorder received a track-related error message.

parameterdescription.para: Handle is a MediaRecorder instance on which callback was set.
to MLMediaRecorderError.
Media Recorder Callback structure defined by MLMediaRecorderEventCallbacks that needs to be initialized.

briefdescription.para: APIs for MediaMuxer to facilitate muxing of elementary streams.

detaileddescription.para: APIs for video and auido recording.

Shared Object:\n media_muxer.magicleap
THIS API IS DEPRECATED IN FAVOR OF STANDARD ANDROID SDK AND NDK MEDIA APIs: https://developer.android.com/ndk/reference/group/media https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaMuxer
Stringify the error codes returned by these APIs, call MLMediaResultGetString.

Shared Object:\n media_recorder.magicleap
THIS API IS DEPRECATED IN FAVOR OF STANDARD ANDROID SDK MEDIA APIs: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaRecorder
Stringify the error codes returned by these APIs, call MLMediaResultGetString.
